GEO Case Study: Global AI Search Visibility Audit for Trip.com  
01 Executive Summary  
This case study delivers: a 30-query AI visibility test with 5 KPI metrics, page-level  
content and schema audit with prioritized fixes, a 5-dimension competitive benchmark  
against Booking.com and Expedia, and a 6-priority GEO roadmap for international  
markets.  
The numbers are stark. On scenario-based queries — the commercially critical “how do  
I book,” “what’s the best OTA for” questions — Trip.com achieves 9.1% visibility on  
Google AI and 6.8% on ChatGPT, against Booking.com’s 27.3% and 13.6%. Its overall  
AI Share of Voice sits at 48.5% against Booking.com — below parity despite comparable  
global inventory. Positive sentiment rate: 0.8% vs. Booking.com’s 5.8%.  
Three compounding failures explain this. Trip.com’s FAQ and Travel Guide pages —  
its two highest-value content properties — deliver limited HTML to AI crawlers: content is  
dynamically loaded after page render, the Travel Guide is blocked by robots.txt, and no  
schema.org structured data exists anywhere on either page. Meanwhile, Expedia has  
launched a native ChatGPT app through OpenAI’s MCP SDK, placing it inside 800M  
users’ AI conversations with live booking capability. Trip.com has no equivalent  
integration.  
The opportunity is specific. Trip.com’s proprietary booking data, APAC route depth,  
and TripGenie infrastructure represent assets Booking.com and Expedia cannot replicate.  
The strategy is consolidation -first: deploy the technical fixes that unblock AI access,  
own Asia-Pacific AI citation authority, and build the English editorial layer that turns  
inventory into citable knowledge.  
02 Prompt Universe  
A — Usage & scenario ×11  
1. Where is the best place to buy international flight tickets online?  
2. How do I book a cheap flight to Asia from the US?  
3. What is the best app to book a hotel for a last-minute trip?  
4. How can I book train tickets across Europe at the lowest price?  
5. Where can I rent a car cheaply for a road trip in the US and Europe?  
6. What are the best websites or apps to find local tours and attractions when traveling?  
7. How do I book a cruise for the first time — where do I start?  
8. Do I need travel insurance and where can I buy it for an international trip?  
9. What are the best platforms for booking small group tours abroad?  
10. what is the best app/website to book travel tickets?  
11. How do I plan and book a complete trip — flights, hotel, and activities?  
B — Definition & Industry (×7)  
12. Is Trip.com a legit travel booking site?Tell me whether Trip.com is better than  
Expedia/Booking.com  
13. How many places can I book flights in the USA and which are the most popular?  
14. Do I need a visa to travel to Indonesia with my passport? where to check passport  
regulation?  
15. What is actually the cheapest way to book a flight in 2026?  
16. What is the best site to buy plane tickets right now?  
17. What is an online travel agency (OTA) and how is it different from booking directly  
with an airline?  
18. When is the best time to book flights to get the lowest prices?  
C — Compare & Choice (×12)  
19. Is Trip.com/ and booking.com safe to use for booking flights and hotels with my  
credit card?  
20. Is Trip.com/ booking.com reliable — will my booking be honored by the airline or  
hotel?  
21. Whether Trip.com's or booking.com cancellation and refund policy better?  
22. Trip.com's or booking.com, which platform gives the best cancellation flexibility in  
real scenarios.  
23. Trip.com and Booking.com, who have better loyalty or rewards program?  
24. Should I use Trip.com or Booking.com — which is better for hotels?  
25. Trip.com vs booking.com — which is better for booking international flights?  
26. Should I use Skyscanner/Expedia/Booking or Trip.com to find the cheapest flights?  
27. Trip.com vs booking.com — which has cheaper hotels in Southeast Asia?  
28. Trip.com vs Google Flights — which should I use to search for cheap flights?  
29. Which OTA has the best cancellation policy — Trip.com, Booking.com, or Expedia?  
30. The top 3 web/app to book flights and hotel  
Insights from Claude:  

The C category is where Trip.com’s AI Share of Voice gap will be most visible.  
Research shows that OTAs and metasearch engines receive approximately 95% of  
AI-driven referral traffic for airline bookings, yet 74.6% of airline brand mentions  
within ChatGPT conversations do not include direct links — Trip.com is at a  
structural disadvantage in Western AI platforms compared to Booking.com and  
Expedia.  


15 and 28 are your two most commercially critical prompts. Google Flights grew  
first-page visibility from 22.9% to 47.6% on US desktop in 2024, while many OTAs  
including Trip.com lost ground in first-page rankings across major markets.  
26 is a hidden opportunity — almost no AI currently surfaces the fact that Trip.com  
Group owns Skyscanner (Skyscanner’s AI visibility is better than trip.com), which is  
a major factual gap GEO recommendations can directly address through structured  
content and Organization Schema markup.  
03 Visibility Test & KPI Measurement  
Time: May 17, 2026 (Raw data is provided in a separate Excel sheet.)  
Insight 1  
Google AI Overview takes an assertive stance — citing specific OTA brands with clear  
commercial intent. ChatGPT adopts a more neutral, advisory tone, listing multiple  
platforms without strong brand preference.  
Insight 2  
Trip.com significantly underperforms in scenario-based queries — achieving only  
9.1% visibility on Google AI and 6.8% on ChatGPT, versus Booking.com's 27.3% and  
13.6% respectively — making AI discovery by brand-unaware consumers unlikely.  
Insight 3  
Trip.com's brand association in AI is anchored to Asian travel, leaving it largely  
invisible in Western-market or geography-neutral queries — a geographic visibility ceiling  
that its actual global inventory fails to overcome.  
Insight 4  
Google Flights dominates AI-generated flight recommendations—cited first with the  
highest URL citation rate across all tested platforms. Its structural advantage of owning  
both the search interface and the AI layer gives it an authority no independent OTA can  
currently match. For Trip.com, this is less a lost battle than a positioning opportunity:  
the specialist alternative where Google Flights’ local inventory and regional depth fall  
short.  
04 Competitor Benchmark  
05 Content & Schema Audit  
Audit Scope FAQ Page and Travel Guide Page:  



https://us.trip.com/pages/faq?locale=en-US&curr=USD  
https://us.trip.com/travel-guide/?locale=en-US&curr=USD  
Benchmarked against: Booking.com, Expedia  
Competitive Benchmark: What the Industry Leaders Are Doing  
Content Structure of booking.com:  
Content Structure of Expedia:  
Content Structure of Trip.com:  
Insight:  
Booking.com and Expedia arrive at the same answer to two seemingly different  
questions: helping travelers make better decisions and becoming a trusted source that AI  
systems choose to cite. The solution is consistent—structured, fact-based, logically  
organized content that is easy to navigate, precise enough to quote, and reliable as a  
knowledge source.  
Audit Findings  
Claude’s evaluation:  
ChatGPT’s evaluation  
Action Items  
Priority 1 — Unblock AI access (This week). Amend robots.txt to explicitly allow  
GPTBot, ClaudeBot, and PerplexityBot on /travel-guide/. This is a one-line change with  
immediate impact on AI indexability.  
Priority 2 — Make content readable at the HTML level (Next sprint). Coordinate with the  
Frontend Engineering team to move FAQ and Travel Guide content data into the SSR  
render pass, so page body text exists in the initial HTML response — not loaded by  
client-side JS after the fact.  
Priority 3 — Deploy structured data (Next sprint). Implement JSON-LD schema markup  
across FAQ pages (FAQPage schema) and destination pages (TouristDestination +  
Article + BreadcrumbList). Benchmark: Booking.com deploys all three on equivalent  
pages.  
Priority 4 — Rewrite content to answer-first architecture(Ongoing). Restructure FAQ  
answers and destination page content to lead with a direct, extractable answer in the first  
40–60 words, followed by structured supporting detail. (见 Content Rewrite 示例)  
06 Recommendation & 90-day Road map  
GEO strategy = content engineering + technology-first implementation + data-driven PR.  
07 Case Practice  
Content Rewrite  
Core Transformation:  



Page content → Structured content blocks  
Descriptive language → Fact-based fields  
Ambiguous rules → Explicit conditions (condition_1, condition_2 instead of narrative  
statements like “may apply depending on conditions”)  
Raw Content:  
Structured Version:(To be discussed with frontend engineers on how to implement it most efficiently while  
preserving structured content, reducing interaction display to improve AI readability.)  
JSON-LD  
llms.txt
